channel.title: flove making-of — Native questions
channel.description: New Native design questions from flove interviews.

item.title: Native circle storage / replication?
item.description:
• OR-Set CRDT over gossipsub — Membership as a CRDT gossiped over libp2p — reuses flovenet's existing CRDT+gossipsub stack; content encrypted to a group key. • Dedicated group protocol — A purpose-built group/channel subsystem; cleaner, more to build. • Reuse social_protocol + storage — Layer circles on existing types; least code, not first-class.
★ decided: OR-Set CRDT over gossipsub

item.title: Group key when a member is removed?
item.description:
• Rotate on removal (forward secrecy) — Re-seal a fresh key to remaining members so the removed peer can't read future content. • Static group key — One key for life; removed members keep it. No forward secrecy. • Rotate on schedule + on removal — Also epoch-rotate; most secure, most overhead.
★ decided: Rotate on removal (forward secrecy)

item.title: Encrypted circle posting via the oasis/SSB add-on (7-recps cap)?
item.description:
• Private groups (box2) per circle — Each circle is an SSB private group with a shared key; scales past the ~7-recipient box1 limit. • box1 recps everywhere — Classic recps ≤7; breaks for real circles. • Re-encrypt per member — A copy per recipient; heavy and leaky.
★ decided: Private groups (box2) per circle

item.title: SSB public posts are irreversible — UI?
item.description:
• Treat public normally — Public is just another audience choice, no special warning. • Explicit 'permanent & public' confirm — Going public names the irreversibility; reinforces private-first. • Public = separate explicit mode — A distinct 'publish to the world' action, separated from private sharing.
★ decided: Treat public normally

item.title: Lineage tree storage?
item.description:
• Append-only signed records over gossipsub — Each co-signed edge gossiped + stored; tree reconstructed from edges. Immutable, traceable. (Default — not explicitly chosen.) • CRDT tree structure — Tree as a CRDT; lineage is immutable history, so append-only fits better. • Kademlia DHT records — Edges as DHT entries; weaker for full traversal.
★ decided: Append-only signed records over gossipsub

item.title: Invite-budget over-issuance with no central counter?
item.description:
• Accept eventual-consistency drift — Don't hard-block; tolerate brief over-issuance under partition, reconcile later by slashing. Fits a P2P network. • Publicly-derivable allowance + detectable over-issuance — Anyone counts issued vs allowance; excess rejected, over-issuer slashed. • Invitee checks before accepting — Each joiner verifies the inviter's count first.
★ decided: Accept eventual-consistency drift
